project_overview.project_name: Somalia Capacity Advancement, Livelihoods and Entrepreneurship, through Digital Uplift Project (SCALED-UP)
project_overview.project_development_objective: Support progress towards increased access to basic digital financial and government services targeting entrepreneurship and employment, particularly for women
project_overview.total_financing: US$31 million (SDR 12.9 million IDA Grant + US$13 million Somalia Multi-Partner Fund)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Residents of Somalia, especially women and female-owned MSMEs; FGS institutions (MoF, MoIFAR, MoCI, MPTT, CBS, FRC, NCA); banks and non-bank financial institutions; Federal Member States

geographic_scope.coverage_type: national with focus on urban areas and areas with security clearance
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - entire country is fragile and conflict-affected; Al Shabaab controls some areas; security restrictions limit project activities in certain locations

key_components.description: Support to CBS, FRC, MPTT/NCA, and new ID Authority for regulatory capacity, financial sector development, ICT regulation, and digital ID system establishment. Includes RegTech solutions, policy development, and institutional capacity building.

key_components.component_name: Component 2: Enabling Financial and Digital Services
key_components.description: Establishment of MSME Financing Facility (US$15M line of credit), digital ID enrollment (1 million people), government and retail payment services interoperability, and e-business registration services through one-stop shop.
Project Implementation Unit staffing, Project Steering Committee support, monitoring and evaluation, grievance redress mechanism, and coordination across implementing agencies.

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Finance (MoF), Federal Republic of Somalia
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee (PSC) headed by MoF with representatives from MoCI, MPTT, MoIFAR, CBS, FRC, NCA, and new ID Authority; Project Implementation Unit (PIU) in MoF with focal persons from key MDAs; Financial sector sub-committee for MSME Financing Facility
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank New Procurement Framework; World Bank Standard Procurement Documents; prior and post review based on risk; STEP system for procurement transactions; special procurement arrangements for FCV country status
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: PIU M&E Unit with MIS; autonomous management bodies for MSME Financing Facility and digital ID services; quarterly progress reports from MSME Financing Facility; independent monitoring agent for MSME Financing Facility; citizen engagement surveys; midterm review at end of year 2

identified_risks.risk_description: Risk of political instability, constitutional review process, AMISOM transition, federal elections in 2020, state-level elections, and tensions between FGS and FMS could slow or derail project implementation, especially digital ID activities
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Policy dialogue and client engagement through World Bank operations; support to intergovernmental institutional frameworks; capacity building to stabilize core government functions; inclusive policy dialogue structures

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited fiscal space, structural over-reliance on grant financing, vulnerability to external shocks (drought, disease, trade bans), dependence on remittances and few commodity exports
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Support for domestic resource mobilization; MSME Financing Facility to fund expansion in productive sectors; annual adjustment of MSME pricing to market conditions; EU budget support operation to provide fiscal space

identified_risks.risk_description: Inadequate capacity in public sector and core institutions; majority of supported institutions are new or yet to be established; delays in setting up MSME Financing Facility and ID Authority; changes in operating environment
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Additional capacity building efforts; external support from development partners; independent monitoring agent; policy commitments from Prime Minister (January 22, 2019); phased and iterative approach; World Bank implementation support

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak PFM legal framework; fragmented accountability systems; high banking and funds flow risks; potential for fraud, corruption, and money laundering; weak domestic banking system
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: EAFS Unit in MoF for FM responsibility; robust internal controls and procedures; external audit by Auditor General; independent monitoring agent; digital payments to reduce leakages; strong FM arrangements with qualified accountants; periodic audits

identified_risks.risk_description: Technical components of digital ID being delivered by NADRA before comprehensive policy and legal framework developed; risk of unsuitable features coded into system; potential non-delivery if Pakistan government financing stops; vendor lock-in risks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Tripartite Technical Working Group (FGS, NADRA, World Bank) for ongoing technical advice; in-depth assessment of technical components; phased approach providing flexibility; World Bank convening role for additional financing if needed; policy framework development before full deployment

identified_risks.risk_description: Telecommunications companies concerned about regulation affecting profits; social resistance to government ID registration; security concerns about holding government-issued ID; fear of extremist groups targeting ID registration points
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Policy dialogue and inclusive consultation; depoliticization of digital ID initiative; continuous citizen engagement and outreach; emphasis on financial access benefits; security risk mitigation through careful implementation strategy

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential adverse environmental impacts from livestock sector financing; weak safeguards capacity of client and beneficiaries; mixed safeguards performance of predecessor projects; social risks including weak labor practices, child/forced labor, occupational health/safety; GBV risks from improved women's financial autonomy
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Focus on two subsectors (livestock and off-grid renewable energy); E&S management consultant in PIU; World Bank safeguards supervision; participation agreements with PFIs including non-discrimination clauses; E&S screening criteria; GBV risk identification and mitigation measures; grievance redress mechanisms

identified_risks.risk_description: Fragile security situation affecting staff safety, implementation timelines, and project budgets; all activities require security threat analysis; potential restrictions on activities in certain locations; extremist groups may target ID registration or threaten ID holders
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Continuous security assessment; flexible project design to adapt to changing security context; high level of planning and coordination; duty of care responsibilities; depoliticization of digital ID; emphasis on financial access narrative

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Project-level GRM established; World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) available; World Bank Inspection Panel available; GRM accessible to women through voice-based messaging and call centers; GBV-sensitive reporting channels integrated
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender gap analysis conducted; women face discrimination in financial access and employment; 50% female enrollment target for digital ID; 30% lending quota for female-owned MSMEs; gender-themed workshops for financial service providers; women's outreach strategies required from PFIs; all-female registration units for ID enrollment; targeted citizen engagement campaigns for women; gender-disaggregated data collection

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project explicitly designed for FCV context; addresses access barriers that may exacerbate political tensions; supports formalization and economic opportunities to reduce vulnerability; depoliticization of digital ID initiative; inclusive governance structures; citizen engagement to build trust in institutions; security-conscious implementation; flexible design to adapt to changing security context
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Somalia remains fragile despite progress since 2011; limited fiscal space constrains service delivery; low trust in state; widespread poverty (69%); high youth unemployment (74% for women, 61% for men); decades of conflict destroyed institutions; Al Shabaab insurgency; recurrent drought and external shocks; limited domestic revenue (2% of GDP); dependence on remittances and aid; weak formal institutions; informal economy dominance
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: All development initiatives require continuous security threat analysis; staff movements limited by security restrictions; some locations may be excluded from activities; project activities may be delayed or cancelled due to security; AMISOM transition and security sector reform ongoing; extremist groups may target ID registration or threaten ID holders; security risk mitigation through careful implementation and narrative framing
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Internally Displaced Persons (IDPs) are significant population; estimated 9% of population displaced; female-headed households more common among IDPs (54%); project targets IDPs for digital ID enrollment; special measures for IDP access to financial services; WFP has registered 4 million people including IDPs; project will leverage humanitarian agency experience with vulnerable populations
